title: Students and firms connect
description: GEORGE NEWS - On Monday night, 27 May, BCom...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - On Monday night, 27 May, BCom students from the Nelson Mandela University (NMU) George Campus had the opportunity to meet and interact exclusively with audit and accounting firms and the business community during the annual Student-Firm Connect event.

The main hall of the Mopani building on the campus was abuzz when the evening kicked off with a social meet-and-greet followed by a round of 'speed dating' between students and firms.


> Afterwards students could return to the firm(s) of their choice for a proper introduction and information gathering on prospective employers and the world of work.


This is an annual event which has in the past led to a number of students finding job placements with local firms upon completion of their studies.

The event is hosted by the Department of Accounting of the local campus.

The annual awards hand-over to the top students of 2023 concluded the evening.

The overall top students were:

1st Year Extended BCom - Kabelo Matena

2nd Year Extended BCom - Sinoyolo Qumba

1st Year BCom Accounting- Ulrich Potgieter

2nd Year BCom Accounting - Kayla van der Merwe

3rd Year BCom Accounting - Lize-Mari Booyens

Peter Brodrick, coordinator of the George Campus Accounting Sciences Department, thanks the various sponsors for their contributions that played a large part in the success of the evening.

PHOTO GALLERY: B Com students connect with business

From the left are Ntanolobuzo Ncenjono, Tsnenolo Matsheka and Alutho Mavuka at the Boshoff Visser Chartered Accountants stall. Keenan Britz and Grant Spaight (far right) chatted to the students.

Students moving between the business stalls.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
